title: STUDY THEME,  The Humanity of Jesus. Part 31
devotional:
3. The Ascension dignifies our humanity.

Would you be surprised if I told you that a human being is the ruler of the universe?

When Jesus sat on the throne of heaven he lost nothing of his humanity. He could have discarded his humanity, but maybe Jesus likes being human. He is God, and he has all the attributes of The Devine, but he is as human as he was on earth. In His humanity he still identifies with our humanity.

Some imagine that Christ shed himself of his humanity and returned to being a completely spiritual being when he went back to Heaven. But this is false. The ascension creates a paradigm shift. The truth is not that Jesus became a baby at Christmas, and then 33 years later came the resurrection and ascension and then he stopped being a man. Jesus is still as human today as he was on earth. He still has a male body with all it distinctive parts. The resurrection demonstrates the incarnation is permanent not temporary. See 1 Timothy 2:5. Philippians 2:5-8.

Jesus retains a human body and we will be like him. And that means that like him, we will have bodies for eternity. We will be transformed, we will be different, but we will still have bodies. Our eternal future will be a physical, bodily future. It is very common for Christians to pit the ‘spiritual’ against the ‘physical’, as if the spiritual were somehow more holy than the physical. We think of redemption in terms of escaping from our bodies. Some Christians are Gnostic in their thinking, they believe that our bodies are contemptible or to be despised. A lot of psychological issues people have regarding their bodies are rooted in Gnostic theology which says that we are just people trapped in a body, and that someday we will be body free. To dismiss our bodies is to think umbilically. Listen to these words from Hebrews 10:5 ‘Therefore, when Christ came into the world, he said: "Sacrifice and offering you did not desire, but a body you prepared for me;’’ In other words the bodies we have been given have been bespokely designed to complement God’s will for our lives.

4. The ascension ensures that we have the authority to be the people God wants us to be.

In the day of Jesus, a victorious king would give gifts to his people to celebrate his victory and enthronement. Acts 1:8 says:-

‘‘But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes on you; and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, and in all Judea and Samaria, and to the ends of the earth.” The gift of the Holy Spirit given to the church by King Jesus is to empower them to be his ‘witnesses.’

The word ’witness,’ is a title given by God, to his servant in the book of Isaiah. If you look at how Isaiah describes a ‘witness’ it has more to do with the quality of the persons relationship with God, than what he does.

Isaiah 43:10

“You are my witnesses,” declares the Lord, “and my servant whom I have chosen, that you may know and believe me and understand that I am he. Before me no god was formed, nor shall there be any after me.’

Isaiah 44:8

Fear not, nor be afraid; have I not told you from of old and declared it? And you are my witnesses! Is there a God besides me? There is no Rock; I know not any.”

The transforming power of the Spirit in our lives give us the godly character needed in order to be authentic witnesses for Jesus. Any witness for Jesus that does not flow from a character that is growing in holiness, because of the ongoing transformation of the Holy Spirit is a false witness.

Bible reading in a year reading plan: - Joshua 5:1-8:23
prayer: Father thank you for showing us that our bodies are something to be celebrated.

Bible reading in a year reading plan: - Joshua 5:1-8:23

Prayer: Father thank you for showing us that our bodies are something to be celebrated.
